title: What to know about Sambucus nigra before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Sambucus nigra, commonly known as elder, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. It is traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of the common cold, flu, and sinusitis, as well as reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with arthritis. The main health benefits of Sambucus nigra include its antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. The herb's therapeutic properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids. Elderberries are typically made into a syrup or wine to utilize the herb's medicinal properties and are often used as a remedy for respiratory issues and as a natural antiviral agent.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, Zingiber officinale has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory properties which can reduce swelling and alleviate pain, as evidenced by its ability to reduce carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Additionally, it has been shown to exhibit antipyretic properties by reducing yeast-induced fever in rats and hypoglycaemic properties by lowering blood glucose levels in rabbits. Furthermore, the extract has been found to have antimicrobial properties, effectively inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria in a dose-dependent manner.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Sambucus nigra are relieving symptoms of the common cold and cough due to its high content of anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Sambucus nigra also finds application in treating sore throat, flu, and sinusitis due to its high concentrations of saponins, phenolic acids, and essential oils that exhibit antimicrobial and mucolytic activities. The plant's ability to reduce fever is attributed to its saponin content, which has been shown to have antipyretic properties. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra is effective in treating respiratory infections, including those caused by viruses and bacteria, due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Additionally, Sambucus nigra has been traditionally used to alleviate diarrhea, allergies, and other respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

uses_list.description: It is used for the common cold because it contains compounds such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have antiviral properties that may help alleviate symptoms and reduce the duration of the illness.
It is used for sore throat due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm the inflamed tissue in the throat, reducing pain and discomfort.
It is used for the flu because it contains compounds that have immunomodulatory effects, which can help stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity and duration of the illness.
It is used to help reduce fever due to its antipyretic properties, which can help lower the body temperature and alleviate discomfort associated with fever.
It is used for coughs because it contains compounds that have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
It is used for sinusitis because it contains compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat infection in the sinuses.
It is used to alleviate allergy symptoms such as congestion and sneezing due to its antihistamine properties, which can help reduce the release of histamine and alleviate associated symptoms.

It is used for respiratory infections because it contains compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat infection in the respiratory tract.
It is used to help alleviate diarrhea due to its astringent properties, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of diarrhea by slowing down bowel movements and firming up stool.

It is used for respiratory issues because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Sambucus nigra is used to treat various respiratory conditions, with its elderberry extract being a key component in alleviating symptoms of the common cold, reducing the severity and duration of sore throat, and exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms. Its antiviral properties make it an effective treatment for sinusitis, flu, and influenza, while its expectorant properties help relieve coughs. Sambucus nigra has also been shown to reduce the severity of bronchitis symptoms and alleviate hay fever symptoms. Furthermore, its antihistamine properties make it a potential treatment for allergic rhinitis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties also help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Sambucus nigra is used to treat fever, a condition often associated with the common cold and influenza. Sambucus nigra has also been used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, which is characterized by a persistent cough. The plant's antiviral properties make it effective in treating sore throat, a common symptom of cold and flu, as well as sinusitis, an inflammation of the nasal passages. Additionally, Sambucus nigra has been used to treat diarrhea, a symptom often associated with the common cold and influenza, and is also used to alleviate cold and flu symptoms. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra's antiviral properties make it an effective treatment for influenza, a highly contagious respiratory illness.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Sambucus nigra is used to treat a variety of respiratory and infectious conditions, commonly including the common cold, a symptom complex characterized by sneezing, runny nose, and fatigue. The plant's antiviral properties also make it effective in treating sore throat, a painful symptom often associated with the flu. Sambucus nigra is also used to help reduce fever, a rise in body temperature that can be caused by the influenza virus. Additionally, the plant's expectorant properties make it useful in treating cough, a symptom that can be associated with respiratory infections. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra is used to treat sinusitis, bronchitis, allergies, and tonsillitis, all conditions that can benefit from the plant's anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Sambucus nigra is used to treat fever, common cold, and cough by virtue of its antipyretic, antiviral, and expectorant properties. It is also utilized to alleviate the symptoms of influenza, sore throat, and diarrhea due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory actions. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra is applied to manage respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, by facilitating mucus expulsion and reducing inflammation in the airways. Additionally, its antimalarial properties make it effective in treating malaria, and it is also employed to combat respiratory infections by boosting the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. By virtue of its broad-spectrum antiviral and anti-inflammatory actions, Sambucus nigra is a valuable herb in unani medicine for treating various conditions.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Sambucus nigra is used to treat various health conditions. Sambucus nigra is commonly used to alleviate the symptoms of influenza, such as fever and body aches, by stimulating the immune system and reducing inflammation. The plant is also effective in treating the common cold, particularly when used in combination with other homeopathic remedies to alleviate congestion and cough. It is also used to treat sore throat, cough, and sinusitis, as well as migraine and tonsillitis, often in combination with other remedies to promote healing and reduce pain. Additionally, Sambucus nigra has antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating ear infections and respiratory infections.
benefits_description: Sambucus nigra boosts immune system by activating macrophages and natural killer cells, thereby enhancing the body's defense mechanism. Sambucus nigra fights respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and relaxing airway smooth muscles. The antipyretic properties of Sambucus nigra reduce fever quickly by inducing sweating and vasodilation, thus lowering body temperature. Sambucus nigra eases cold symptoms by inhibiting the replication of viral and bacterial pathogens and suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The antiviral and antimicrobial properties of Sambucus nigra soothe coughs instantly, relieve congestion issues, treat sore throats, prevent flu outbreaks, combat infections rapidly, and shorten illness duration.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains bioflavonoids and other compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and diseases.
Its antiviral and antimicrobial properties help to combat respiratory infections such as the common cold and flu, reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways.
The plant's bioflavonoids and other compounds have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with high temperatures.
Its antiviral and antimicrobial properties, along with its ability to stimulate the immune system, help to alleviate symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and coughing.
The plant's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughing.
Its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause sore throats, reducing inflammation and alleviating pain.
The plant's antiviral properties help to prevent the spread of the flu virus, reducing the risk of outbreaks and complications.
Its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing instant relief from coughing and congestion.
Its antimicrobial and antiviral properties help to combat infections rapidly, reducing the risk of complications and shortening the duration of illness.
The plant's ability to stimulate the immune system and combat infections rapidly helps to shorten the duration of illness, allowing the body to recover faster.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Sambucus nigra supports cardiac function by exerting antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress, which is a major contributor to cardiac damage. This plant regulates blood pressure through its vasodilatory action, caused by flavonoids and glycosides that relax blood vessels, thereby improving circulation flow and reducing blood clotting. The reduction in blood clotting also reduces the inflammatory response, which is a significant contributor to various cardiovascular diseases. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra enhances endothelial function by activating endothelial nitric oxide synthase, which boosts the immune system by increasing the production of nitric oxide. Additionally, its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, such as the presence of saponins and phenolic acids, support its ability to improve oxygen delivery to tissues.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Sambucus nigra soften digestive walls by exerting mucilaginous properties, creating a protective barrier against irritants. It protects intestinal tract through its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, preventing the proliferation of pathogens that can cause infections and inflammation. Sambucus nigra reduce gastro inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the digestive tract. The plant treat diarrhea quickly by regulating the movement of gut muscles and enhancing water absorption, preventing excessive fluid loss and dehydration. Additionally, Sambucus nigra relieve constipation symptoms, prevent ulcer formation through its protective barrier, boost gut immunity, soothe irritated esophagus, reduce bloating pressure, and heal gut lining damage due to its mucilaginous, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Sambucus nigra regulates hormone production and metabolism by modulating the endocrine system, primarily through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that disrupt hormone balance. This plant supports thyroid function by providing essential nutrients, including iodine and selenium, which are crucial for thyroid hormone production and prevention of hypothyroidism. Sambucus nigra also balances insulin levels by reducing inflammation in the pancreas, a key factor in the development of insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties stabilize the adrenal glands, which are responsible for producing stress hormones like cortisol, thereby reducing anxiety and fatigue. By reducing cortisol levels, Sambucus nigra protects the pancreas from damage, enhances ovarian function by regulating estrogen production, soothes adrenaline surges, and modulates androgen production, ultimately boosting metabolism and reducing the risk of metabolic disorders.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Sambucus nigra soothes skin irritation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling. Relieving itchy areas is also a benefit of Sambucus nigra, attributed to its antihistamine and anti-itch properties, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, Sambucus nigra repels mosquito bites through its insecticidal properties, preventing bites and the associated itching and swelling. The plant's antimicrobial properties also enable it to heal acne scars by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause acne. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra cures eczema flare-ups with its antifungal and antibacterial properties, while fighting fungal infections and solving skin rashes are also possible thanks to its antifungal properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Sambucus nigra boosts lymphatic flow by stimulating the lymphatic vessels and improving the contraction and relaxation of lymphatic muscles. This increase in lymphatic flow reduces lymphedema swelling by effectively removing excess fluids and proteins from the interstitial space. Improved lymphatic drainage is also facilitated by the antifungal and antimicrobial properties of Sambucus nigra, which help to prevent the growth of pathogens that can clog the lymphatic system. Enhancing immune function is a result of the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of the plant, which help to reduce inflammation levels and increase the production of white blood cells. By supporting lymph node health and stimulating the lymphatic system, Sambucus nigra increases white blood cell count, improves detoxification process, and reduces toxic waste buildup, ultimately contributing to a healthy and balanced immune system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Sambucus nigra reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This reduction in inflammation has a positive effect on the musculoskeletal system, allowing for relaxation of muscles, which is a direct result of its muscle-relaxing properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra also contribute to strengthening bones by enhancing bone mineral density. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra improves flexibility and enhances joint mobility by reducing oxidative stress and promoting the production of collagen, a key component of connective tissue. Additionally, the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Sambucus nigra decrease pain, speed healing, stabilize joints, support tendons, and prevent arthritis, ultimately providing comprehensive support for the musculoskeletal system.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Sambucus nigra calm stress through its flavonoid content, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit antioxidant properties that mitigate stress-inducing free radicals. This plant relieve anxiety by modulating the activity of GABA receptors, increasing the inhibitory neurotransmitter's availability, thus promoting a sense of calmness. Sambucus nigra improve focus by stimulating the release of acetylcholine, a key neurotransmitter involved in attention and memory processing. The plant regulate emotions by modulating the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby reducing the production of stress hormones like cortisol. Sambucus nigra also boost energy by increasing the availability of ATP, the primary energy currency of the body, due to its adaptogenic properties, and promote relaxation by decreasing the heart rate and blood pressure through its sedative effects.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Sambucus nigra supports reproductive health by boosting fertility hormones, which are responsible for its antimicrobial properties that prevent reproductive infections. It regulates menstrual cycles due to its antioxidant properties that protect the reproductive system from oxidative stress. Sambucus nigra also tones reproductive organs, enhances sperm quality, and stimulates ovulation through its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and promote healthy cell growth. Additionally, it eases menstrual cramps and improves fetal development due to its antispasmodic properties that relieve cramping and promote healthy fetal growth. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra reduces menopausal symptoms and improves fertility hormones, which are responsible for its immunomodulatory properties that regulate the immune system and promote healthy reproductive function.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Sambucus nigra relieves congestion due to its vasodilatory properties that increase blood flow and promote the clearance of mucus. The anti-inflammatory properties of Sambucus nigra soothe inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing swelling and allowing for easier breathing. Its expectorant properties help reduce mucus production, making it easier to cough up and expel from the body. Sambucus nigra fights infections with its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens that can cause respiratory illnesses. Its antitussive properties ease coughs, while its bronchodilatory properties prevent asthma, cure sinusitis, treat bronchitis, alleviate wheezing, and combat allergies.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Sambucus nigra prevents infections in the urinary tract due to its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause these infections. The plant reduces inflammation in the urinary system, alleviating symptoms of conditions such as cystitis, by virtue of its anti-inflammatory properties. Sambucus nigra cures UTIs through its ability to inhibit the production of virulence factors in uropathogenic bacteria, thereby preventing them from causing infections. Additionally, it treats prostatitis by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of pathogens in the prostate gland, thanks to its antioxidant properties. Sambucus nigra also relieves nephritis, alleviates pyelonephritis, heals kidney stones, eases bladder spasms, fights kidney damage, and soothes the urinary tract, due to its diuretic, expectorant, and demulcent properties.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Sambucus nigra are multifaceted, exhibiting antimicrobial properties due to the presence of saponins that inhibit the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, while also displaying antioxidant properties as a result of the flavonoids, which help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Sambucus nigra's anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, thereby also demonstrating analgesic effects. The antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra are thought to be mediated by the flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses. Furthermore, the plant's expectorant properties are due to the glycosides, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, while its antihistamine properties are attributed to the flavonoids, which help to alleviate allergic reactions. Additionally, the immunomodulatory properties of Sambucus nigra are believed to be mediated by the flavonoids and glycosides, which help to regulate the immune system and prevent disease.

properties_list.description: Sambucus nigra has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, demonstrating the ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, such as Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus, thereby preventing the development of infections.

Sambucus nigra possesses antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds, which can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, potentially contributing to the prevention of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease.

Sambucus nigra has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, with extracts from the plant exhibiting the ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in various cell cultures, which may be beneficial in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

Sambucus nigra has been traditionally used as an analgesic, with its extracts demonstrating the ability to reduce pain in various animal models, possibly due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can interact with pain receptors and modulate pain perception.

Sambucus nigra has been found to exhibit antiviral properties, with extracts from the plant demonstrating the ability to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, such as the influenza virus, which may be beneficial in the prevention and treatment of viral infections.

Sambucus nigra has been traditionally used as an expectorant, with its extracts demonstrating the ability to facilitate the removal of mucus and other respiratory secretions, potentially contributing to the relief of symptoms associated with respiratory tract infections and other conditions.

Sambucus nigra has been found to exhibit antihistamine properties, with extracts from the plant demonstrating the ability to inhibit the release of histamine and other mediators of allergic reactions, which may be beneficial in the treatment of conditions such as allergic rhinitis and anaphylaxis.

Sambucus nigra has been found to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, with extracts from the plant demonstrating the ability to modulate the immune response and enhance the production of antibodies and other immune factors, which may be beneficial in the prevention and treatment of infections and other conditions.

constituents.description: Flavonoids are present in Sambucus nigra and have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Saponins are a type of triterpenoid glycoside found in Sambucus nigra, characterized by their foaming properties, emulsifying capabilities, and potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
Phenolic acids are water-soluble compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Quercetin is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.
Tannins in Sambucus nigra are astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial compounds
Coumarins are a type of lignan, possessing antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties.
Sambucus nigra contains minerals including calcium, iron, magnesium, manganese, potassium, and zinc.

Triterpenes are a class of compounds found in Sambucus nigra, characterized by their triterpene skeleton structure and diverse chemical properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
Kaempferol is a flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been found to have antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal activities.
Anthocyanins are water-soluble pigments with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potentially antimicrobial properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Sambucus nigra are flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, while also possessing antimicrobial properties. The plant also contains saponins, which have been reported to inhibit the growth of certain pathogenic microorganisms and to exhibit antiviral activity. Additionally, Sambucus nigra contains a range of phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Tannins are also present, contributing to the plant's astringent and antimicrobial activities, and flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. These medicinal constituents work in concert to contribute to the plant's traditional use as an antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Sambucus nigra for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves, rich in saponins and volatile oils, possess antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, contributing to their use in treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The combination of flavonoids and saponins in Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves is believed to contribute to their immunomodulatory and antiviral activities. The primary medicinal properties of these parts are attributed to their ability to modulate the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral effects.

parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, primarily benefiting cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exerting immunomodulatory effects and anti-cancer activities.

The leaves of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, primarily used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as exhibiting antiviral and anti-cancer effects, and supporting immune system function.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues, as well as to boost immune system function.
preparations.overview: Sambucus nigra tincture is widely used to treat common cold, sore throat, sinusitis, bronchitis, flu, tonsillitis, allergies, cough, hay fever and influenza. It is one of the most commonly used herbal remedies due to its effectiveness in alleviating respiratory issues. The primary effect of Sambucus nigra tincture is its ability to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages and lungs, making it easier to breathe. This preparation is typically made with the flowers and leaves of the Sambucus nigra plant, which are mixed with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated liquid extract. The process of making Sambucus nigra tincture is moderately complex, requiring precise measurement and steeping of the plant material, but it is a common practice in herbal medicine.
recipe: Gather 1 part Sambucus nigra flowers and 1 part leaves in a clean glass jar.
Fill a clean glass jar with 80-100 proof vodka and pour over the Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, making sure they are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure the botanicals and vodka are well combined.
After the steeping process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place; recommended dosage is 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, and to reduce fever and inflammation.
preparations.overview: Sambucus nigra salve is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including sore throat, cold sores, cough, common cold, boils, skin irritation, bronchitis, herpes, fungal infections, and eczema. It's a widely used remedy, often found in homes and first-aid kits due to its effectiveness. This preparation has a common effect of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Sambucus nigra salve is typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves of the elder tree in a carrier oil, creating a salve that can be applied topically to affected areas. The process of making this salve is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbalism and the right equipment to extract the medicinal properties from the plant material.
recipe: Harvest 1 cup of Sambucus nigra flowers and 1/2 cup of leaves, making sure they are free of pesticides and contaminants.
Dry the flowers and leaves in a warm, dark place for 1-2 weeks, or until they reach a crumbly consistency.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried flowers and leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the mixture reaches the desired consistency and color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

preparations.description: Elixir made from this plant is used to treat fever, cough, sore throat, and respiratory infections, particularly in children, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.overview: Sambucus nigra elixir is used to treat common cold, sore throat, sinusitis, flu, bronchitis, allergies, tonsillitis, cough, hay fever, and influenza. It's a widely used herbal preparation. Sambucus nigra elixir has an antiviral effect, inhibiting the replication of viruses in the body. The preparation is typically made from the flowers of Sambucus nigra, also known as the black elder tree. The process of making Sambucus nigra elixir is labor-intensive and requires precise extraction of bioactive compounds from the flowers, making it a challenging task for manufacturers.
recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers with 1 liter of 70% ethanol in a clean glass container, ensuring the flowers are completely submerged.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily to aid infusion.
Strain the elixir through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the Sambucus nigra herbal elixir in a dark glass bottle, label it, and take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat colds, flu, fever, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its antiviral and expectorant properties.
preparations.overview: Sambucus nigra tea is a herbal preparation used to treat common cold, sore throat, sinusitis, bronchitis, allergies, flu, cough, tonsillitis, hay fever, and influenza. It's one of the most commonly used herbal remedies worldwide. The effect of taking Sambucus nigra tea is usually a reduction in severity and duration of symptoms. The preparation is made by infusing the flowers of the elder tree in hot water. Making Sambucus nigra tea is relatively simple and requires only a few minutes to prepare, as the flowers can be steeped in hot water for a short period of time.
recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers from a trusted source.
Clean and rinse the flowers to remove any dirt or debris.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan over low-medium heat.
Steep the Sambucus nigra flowers in the heated water for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed, and at the recommended dosage of 1-2 cups per day.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used for the treatment of constipation, hemorrhoids, and to induce labor in pregnant women.
preparations.overview: Sambucus nigra suppository is used to treat influenza, constipation, sore throat, diarrhea, sinusitis, common cold, cough, bronchitis, gastroenteritis and cold. This preparation is uncommonly used, possibly due to the availability of conventional treatments. The suppository has a unique effect of acting locally in the rectum. It is made from the flowers of Sambucus nigra, which are collected and processed into a suppository form. The manufacturing process is complex and requires specialized equipment, making mass production difficult.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Sambucus nigra for medicinal purposes are tinctures, often used to treat fever and respiratory issues, such as colds and flu. Salves are commonly applied topically to relieve pain and inflammation associated with skin conditions like eczema and acne. Elixirs are also prepared to alleviate symptoms of allergies and sinusitis. A tea can be consumed to soothe sore throats and coughs, often in the form of a warm infusion. Suppositories are occasionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Consuming the berries of Sambucus nigra causes nausea due to the presence of glycosides, particularly sambunigrin and sambunine, which have emetic properties.
Consuming large quantities of Sambucus nigra fruit causes emesis due to the toxic compounds ellagic acid and saponins.
Symptoms include severe abdominal cramps and diarrhea after consuming Sambucus nigra leaves, flowers, or berries.
Sambucus nigra extracts contain anthraquinones, which can cause gastrointestinal symptoms including diarrhea.

Sambucus nigra can cause a significant reduction in blood pressure in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or in combination with other blood pressure-lowering medications.
Sambucus nigra extracts can cause a decrease in heart rate due to vagal stimulation.
Symptoms include hives, itching, swelling, and difficulty breathing after exposure to Sambucus nigra.
Decreased respiratory rate and depth caused by excessive Sambucus nigra consumption
Ingestion of Sambucus nigra berries has been associated with the risk of seizures, particularly in children.
Cyanosis is caused by severe Sambucus nigra leaf and stem contact with the skin.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Sambucus nigra are nausea and vomiting, which can cause abdominal pain and diarrhea due to gastrointestinal irritation, potentially leading to hypotension, a drop in blood pressure. Bradycardia, a slow heart rate, is also a possible effect, as the plant's compounds can interfere with cardiac function. An allergic reaction can occur, especially in individuals with a history of allergies, and if severe, it can lead to respiratory depression, a life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention. Seizures can also occur, particularly in individuals with a predisposition to seizure disorders, and in severe cases, seizures can progress to cyanosis, a condition characterized by a bluish discoloration of the skin and mucous membranes.

precautions.precaution_description: The flowers, seeds, and leaves of Sambucus nigra contain glycosides which can cause cardiac arrest, and a healthcare professional should be consulted before using the plant.
Conduct patch tests or ask about known allergies before using Sambucus nigra products.
Sambucus nigra contains emodin, a lignan that can stimulate uterine contractions, potentially inducing premature labor.
Sambucus nigra contains compounds that inhibit lactation, potentially affecting milk production.
Sambucus nigra may lower blood sugar levels in people with diabetes due to its effect on insulin secretion and glucose metabolism.
Elevated blood pressure can be caused by Sambucus nigra due to berberine content.
Sambucus nigra can lower blood sugar levels, exacerbating diabetes.
Sambucus nigra may lower blood sugar levels, exacerbating hypoglycemia.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Sambucus nigra medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to discuss safe usage and potential interactions with existing medications, determine allergies to this plant as it can cause an allergic reaction similar to ragweed, avoid using it during pregnancy due to potential effects on the fetus, monitor blood pressure as it can cause a drop in blood pressure, and avoid using it with diabetes as it may affect blood sugar levels, also monitor blood sugar levels when using this plant and avoid using it with hypoglycemia as it can exacerbate low blood sugar.
identification: Sambucus nigra is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Adoxaceae family, characterized by its dark green, ovate leaves with 3-5 pairs of leaflets and a serrated margin. It typically grows to a height of 3-6 meters, with a spread of 2-4 meters, and has a woody stem with a grey-brown bark. Native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, this plant is widely distributed across temperate regions, often found in damp woodlands, hedgerows, and riverbanks. It produces small, greenish-yellow flowers in dense, spherical clusters, which give way to dark blue or black berries in late summer, attracting various bird species for food and seed dispersal. The plant's growth habit and geographical distribution make it a common sight in many urban and rural areas across its native range.
cultivation: Sambucus nigra is a deciduous shrub or small tree that requires full sun to partial shade, well-drained soil, and a pH between 6.0 and 7.0 to thrive. It grows best in moist to wet environments, but can tolerate dry conditions when mature, and is often found in riparian zones, hedgerows, and woodlands. To cultivate this plant, it is recommended to provide it with regular watering, especially during its first year, and to prune it annually to maintain its shape and promote healthy growth. Sambucus nigra can grow up to 4 meters tall and wide, and can be propagated through seed, cuttings, or layering. Growth tips include providing support for the plant when young, and mulching around the base to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
history: Sambucus nigra is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Europe and North America. The berries of the plant contain anthocyanins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In the 18th and 19th centuries, Sambucus nigra was used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and indigestion, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant's berries and flowers were also used to make a medicinal tea that was said to have antibacterial and antiviral properties. Historical records suggest that Sambucus nigra was used by herbalists and physicians, including the famous English physician Nicholas Culpeper, who wrote about its medicinal properties in his book 'The Complete Herbal' in 1653.
